description: Whole document open in Compose. Input contract for playbook actions that operate on the entire document (e.g., compose-summarize, full-document compare, document-wide risk scan). The R1 smoke test (compose-summarize) consumes this scope.

fields.name: documentSpeId
fields.description: SharePoint Embedded drive-item id of the open document. The session<>document binding key. Always present (Compose binding rule: every document is in SPE). The BFF dispatch endpoint fetches the DOCX bytes from SPE and extracts text server-side for playbook consumption; only the SPE id crosses the BFF<>UI boundary on this scope.

fields.name: documentVersionEtag
fields.description: SPE driveItem etag at the moment of dispatch. Lets the playbook detect that the document changed mid-flight and reject stale invocations. Optional; if absent the playbook proceeds without staleness verification.

fields.name: documentRecordId
fields.description: sprk_document GUID when the document has been promoted (post-first-Save). Null for ephemeral pre-Save docs (Path B). When present, enables matter-binding lookups + audit trail joins + the normal Document AI Search indexing pipeline.

fields.name: matterId
fields.description: sprk_matter GUID when the document is matter-bound. Null when the document is unbound or pre-Save. When present, enables matter-scoped knowledge retrieval and writes derived insights to the matter's knowledge graph.

fields.name: documentName
fields.description: Display name of the document (typically the SPE driveItem name). Used by the playbook for UI rendering and citation envelopes. Not the source of truth for identity — that's documentSpeId.

fields.name: documentMimeType
fields.description: MIME type of the document (typically 'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document' for DOCX). Carried into RoutingContext.MimeType for content-aware routing (e.g., a future 'summarize-pdf' variant routes off this).

fields.name: sessionId
fields.description: ChatSession id (Redis/Cosmos/Dataverse three-tier persistence key). The playbook writes its summary output as a structured ChatMessage so prior-session continuity (Compose 'prior sessions for this document' UX) preserves the artifact.

fields.name: tenantId
fields.description: Azure AD tenant GUID. ADR-015 Tier 3 isolation key; inherited from PlaybookInvocationContext.TenantId.

inputs.rationale: The scope carries identifiers + names only — no document text. The playbook fetches DOCX bytes from SPE itself; this keeps the scope payload tiny and avoids re-marshalling the document text through the BFF<>UI boundary on every dispatch.

outputs.expected: Playbook-shape: terminal-node text + structured fields per the invoked action's JPS output schema. For compose-summarize (R1 smoke test target), the Document Summary playbook (id 47686eb1-9916-f111-8343-7c1e520aa4df) is expected to produce the existing PB-002 Document Profile output shape: { summary, keyParties, dates, obligations, riskFlags, confidence } per file-summary.json precedent.

datagovernance.note: Document TEXT is fetched server-side by the playbook; it does not transit the scope payload. The scope itself contains only deterministic identifiers (ADR-015 compliant).

metadata.author: spaarkeai-compose-r1 spike-4
metadata.consumedBy: R1 smoke test: compose-summarize → Document Summary playbook (47686eb1-9916-f111-8343-7c1e520aa4df). R2: any whole-document action (e.g., compose-risk-scan, compose-extract-obligations) consumes this scope without retrofit.
